Decision: relax referent-free generation for an MVP

Referent stripping in generation (system prompt and filter) is switched off, and the content mold is broadened, so the pipeline produces a functional, knowledgeable prompt-response model. Other work depends on having a working small language model. Referent-free generation is deferred, to return through a constructed world-state generator rather than through prompt and filter restriction.

The generation system prompt (prompts.py) no longer forbids real facts, named entities, numbers, dates, or technical vocabulary; the filter (filters.py) no longer blocklists real-world words or bans digits, keeping only assistant-framing and meta-commentary detection. Relaxing the restrictions alone left the corpus a monoculture of scenery vignettes, because the seed vocabulary, prose forms, and few-shot exemplars were all landscape material, so a second pass broadened the content mold: seeds.py now samples a wide subject-domain taxonomy (everyday life, work, science, history, arts, relationships, health, technology, food, travel, law, sport, money, ideas, craft, land) instead of nature nouns; prose now demands a plot rather than static observation; a new reasoning text type (node 42) demands strictly ordered explanation or argument; and instruction pairs now span many real task kinds rather than scene descriptions. The severity parameter, which previously selected the entity vocabulary, no longer affects prompts at all and is now inert.

This decision reprioritizes G1 (node 1) below a practical milestone: a working, trainable, evaluable prompt-response model, since multiple other elements of the project are blocked on having one to test against. It is explicitly temporary and does not retract the referent-free premise; the plan is to reintroduce referent-freeness once the MVP has validated the training and evaluation pipeline end to end, and to do so through the persistent world-state and program-as-author architecture (node 36, node 38) rather than by re-tightening prompts.py and filters.py directly, since the earlier prompt-and-filter approach is the mechanism that produced the diversity-contraction and consistency problems (node 34, node 35) in the first place. Two content changes in this pass carry lasting intent independent of the MVP framing: prose should have plot and reasoning text should have real order, both of which were identified as failures of the earlier corpus (fluent but plotless prose, and logic that was easy to fake because it was poetic and unordered). The definition text type was also changed from inventing headwords to defining real words, so the model acquires genuine lexical knowledge during this MVP phase.
